content:
Hi BIAV, 

I think you have already received a lot of feedback regarding Pollux's power level. However, I wanted to focus specifically on some issues with his skills that I think are major problems for him from a design standpoint. Regardless of how powerful he is, I believe these are fundamental issues which affect how he feels to play, meaning that if they are not addressed, he will not feel good to use regardless of how strong he is. Please consider changing or adjusting these parts of his kit.

There are two important points I want to establish first. These two points are crucial to Pollux's character identity, and I believe it is important for his design to stay consistent with these points. These two points are:

1. Pollux is very similar to Castor, which is clearly very intentional. They are twins, and being similar in design is a good choice to emphasize their similarities. Many of his mechanics are very similar to Castor's mechanics, and they are clearly meant to complement each other when played on the same team.

2. Pollux is very clearly designed as a sustained damage character. His cards all have Retain, his Sin Mark mechanic scales up over time, and Atonement by Pain increases in value for every fight in an exploration. All of this, combined, means that his skill design clearly communicates that he is a sustained damage character who ramps up over time. This means that he does not have the ability to do massive, overwhelming burst damage to one-turn kill a boss. Instead, he should make up for it by having a consistent amount of damage every turn, with some capability to do high damage when set up.

These are the two core concepts that I believe define Pollux as a character. However, his kit has many flaws which affect his performance with regards to these concepts. Here are the main issues I believe he has:
thread_title: ✅「Answered」Flaws and Weaknesses in Pollux's skill design

content:
1. "Atonement by Pain" talent does not give Shield. As defined by principle #1, Pollux is very similar to Castor. However, Pollux does not have a reliable source of Shield like Castor does. Castor has Shield on every card he plays (because of Quasar), and his Onyx Plume, Stygian Wing, and Dirge of the Unbroken all provide very high amounts of Shield for defensive ability.

Pollux does not have any source of Shield except for his "Atonement by Pain" talent. However, the Shield increase from "Atonement by Pain" only works on his Defense card. This means that Pollux does not have a consistent Shield available to him. This means he does not follow concept #1, but it also violates concept #2. As a sustained damage character, it is important that there is more focus on defensive ability compared to a high-damage burst character. However, because he has no consistent Shield, Pollux does not have any defensive ability available to him.

My suggestion to fix this issue is that "Atonement by Pain" should provide a Shield on all cards, not only his Defense card. This will allow for consistent Shield gain on Pollux, which will provide defensive utility to better fit his identity as a ramping, sustained damage character. It will also complement and provide more similarity with Castor as they will both have consistent Shield gain.
thread_title: ✅「Answered」Flaws and Weaknesses in Pollux's skill design

content:
2. "Sin Mark" does not interact with any other characters or mechanics, and does not provide enough of a benefit by itself. The idea behind "Sin Mark" makes sense and follows concept #2, because it provides a ramping buildup that makes him stronger over time. However, the problem is that because of #1, most of the damage that Pollux deals actually comes from Castor's "Corrosion". Sin Mark does not apply Bleed based on Corrosion, meaning that the Bleed value obtained from "Sin Mark" is actually very low. Because Pollux scales off of Base DMG and Damage Amplification, his personal damage will not exceed a certain amount. This means that "Sin Mark" ends up not being very useful, because it has no interaction with other characters or other mechanics. Even if you last a long time and build up "Sin Mark" to a high value, the amount of Bleed applied is still very small and does not serve to fulfill the purpose of powering him up signficantly over time.

My suggestion to fix this issue is for "Sin Mark" to interact with other characters in some way. One way to do this is to make "Sin Mark" apply to damage dealt by every character in the team. Another way is for "Sin Mark" to include Corrosion damage when applying Bleed.
thread_title: ✅「Answered」Flaws and Weaknesses in Pollux's skill design

content:
3. The order of play between "Onyx Plume" and "Sacred Heart" does not make sense. Because of the similarities and design principle behind #1, it is clear that Sacred Heart and Onyx Plume are cards that are meant to complement each other. When played together, they have useful effects and work well. However, the way that Sacred Heart is designed means that it must be played before Onyx Plume. This is because Sacred Heart is placed on top of the Draw Pile, and Onyx Plume will draw it. We can also see this design philosophy clearly in the design of Pollux's "Soulsearing Light" skill, as when played together with Castor, it can draw 2 Onyx Plume cards to help combo with Sacred Heart.

However, because of the way that Castor's "Corrosion" works, the order that they must be played in does not make sense. Castor will only apply "Corrosion" after playing Onyx Plume. This means that damage should be applied to an enemy after playing Onyx Plume, as otherwise it will not activate the "Corrosion" effect.

However, Pollux's Sacred Heart must be played before Onyx Plume in order for Onyx Plume to draw it. Additionally, if you do not have Onyx Plume in hand, you must first play Soulsearing Light in order to draw it. This will use up Pollux's "Atonement by Pain" talent, which means that he will have less damage available to activate "Corrosion".

The way this is designed means that they do not actually work together very well. If you play Sacred Heart first, then you will lose Pollux's "Atonement by Pain" effect, which means it is much harder to activate "Corrosion". If you play Onyx Plume first, then you will be wasting a draw which can be used to draw Sacred Heart after playing. Either way, you must choose to sacrifice some ability when using these two together, which means they do not complement each other very well.
thread_title: ✅「Answered」Flaws and Weaknesses in Pollux's skill design

content: I do not have an easy suggestion on how to fix it, but I believe that this is not designed very well since it means that playing these two cards together requires giving up something.
thread_title: ✅「Answered」Flaws and Weaknesses in Pollux's skill design

content:
4. The "Prepare" on Sacred Heart is not very useful and does not work well. This is because after playing Sacred Heart, it is returned to the top of the Draw Pile. However, unless you have a way to draw cards, it will not be added to your hand. This means that the "Prepare" effect will not activate.

The most common ways to draw Sacred Heart after playing them are using Onyx Plume or the Insight card. However, in both cases, you do not want to keep Sacred Heart in hand. If you play Onyx Plume, it will apply Corrosion, which requires more damage to be dealt to activate this. This means you will want to play the Sacred Heart in order to activate Corrosion. If you play Insight, it will give you +1 Arithmetica. This means that if you do not play Sacred Heart, the arithmetica will be wasted. There is little to no reason to keep Sacred Heart in your hand without playing it after you have used a card to draw it.

If you do not use a card to draw it, then Sacred Heart will not be added to your hand until the next turn. However, this means that it will not activate Prepare. Despite Sacred Heart having Prepare, activating it will take 2 full turns: the first turn it will be returned to top of Draw Pile, and then the second turn it will be added to hand and at the end of the turn it will activate Prepare.

This ends up being very pointless, because waiting two turns to activate Prepare is not very useful. Additionally, because it goes back to costing 1 Arithmetica after being played, Pollux's own card "Soulsearing Light" will not draw it. This means that if you are not playing Castor, it is hard to draw it back to hand, making it not a very helpful design.
thread_title: ✅「Answered」Flaws and Weaknesses in Pollux's skill design

content: My suggestion to fix this issue is to activate "Prepare" on Sacred Heart after it is played. This way, it will be reduced to costing 0 Arithmetica when it is drawn, which will make it immediately useful either on the same turn or on the next turn. This way, it will also be obtainable via using Pollux's own Soulsearing Light card, instead of having to rely on Castor or other characters to draw it.
thread_title: ✅「Answered」Flaws and Weaknesses in Pollux's skill design

content:
5. The effect of "Cleansing Judgement" to be added to hand on entering Ultra Turn takes too long to be helpful. Because Ultra Turn can only happen every 5 turns, you cannot add Cleansing Judgement to hand easily except for every 5 turns. This card is not powerful enough that adding it once every 5 turns is useful. It is not consistent enough this way, and does not provide much sustained damage, making it fail to achieve concept #2. 

My suggestion for fixing this is to make it easier to add Cleansing Judgement to hand in some way. For example, Cleansing Judgement can be shuffled back into the Draw Pile after being played (but does not need to be top of Draw Pile, just into Draw Pile will help). Or, Cleansing Judgement can add itself to Ultra Space after being played, so that you can enter Ultra Turn faster.
thread_title: ✅「Answered」Flaws and Weaknesses in Pollux's skill design
✅「Answered」Flaws and Weaknesses in Pollux's skill design

content:
為什麼這麼說呢 我覺得光用菲茵特主線就忘卻星辰98%的關卡不存在難度了 大部分不用靈芝也能速戰速決 而且我全隊常駐且都沒有超限 只有60～62級 絕對沒有數值 全隊都沒有
連一般認為不存在數值和死抗以外任何作用的菲茵特都可以這樣用了，大部分角色應該沒有不夠強的問題吧⋯
thread_title: 💭「处理中」忘卻篇第六章共鳴卡純白之帆 & 第八章共鳴歡愉之理

content:
追求頂級強度是一回事 但我覺得玩遊戲就是體驗玩法機制跟劇情就好 其他都是多的 有也行 沒有也行
而且之後深潛排名改榮譽榜 跟其他玩家也沒有什麼好比較的 當然要私下曬圖討論都行 但對遊戲體驗來說其實差異不大 那要加強什麼呢
如果是更有趣的搭配 更多元的機制 例如日服墨菲一張卡作用次數在一到三之間變化 或是菲茵特更加融入血肉開始可以操作胚胎融合跟猩紅熔爐 這樣的改動我覺得會是很好玩有趣的
但是單純把數值往上調就很沒有必要了
比起一卡破億的隊伍，出牌方法有趣的隊伍 我覺得比較重要
即便沒有特化出很強的出傷 如果具有一定的平衡性 能在絕大部分關卡上獲勝 那就是很好的隊伍了
實際上我的隊伍就是要反擊沒幾層 要盾沒幾層 要力量沒幾層 要戒備從來沒有 爆擊率除了珊都5% 沒有銀鑰手 沒有人產狂氣 胚胎一半以上生吃
但這樣的隊伍也一路走來過了幾十關主線
那我覺得就是足夠好的隊伍了
那麼這種強度只有地下室的隊伍都可以通過的關卡 大部分我都看不出其他角色單純變得數值更高或機制變得「更強」的必要
因為也沒有更艱難的關卡要去克服啊⋯⋯？
thread_title: 💭「处理中」忘卻篇第六章共鳴卡純白之帆 & 第八章共鳴歡愉之理

content:
我願意為了更驚奇的組隊方法去湊角色跟裝備
但如果只是更強 那我覺得沒有什麼意義⋯⋯我已經克服了我需要克服的關卡機制 那麼更高的強度並不能說明任何事情
總之我覺得忘卻比較像是益智遊戲 我只要數值我為什麼不去玩歧路旅人手遊版呢（當然不排除只是我菜逼八不知道他有什麼機制 除了特定大首領大部分都是一頓胖揍能了事的 不能就去提升攻防然後回來一頓胖揍 我玩那個的時候享受的是不一樣的東西）
當我在玩忘卻的時候 我想要的就是每個回合不知道會抽到什麼卡 跟我想出辦法渡過每個回合 這體驗跟感覺
thread_title: 💭「处理中」忘卻篇第六章共鳴卡純白之帆 & 第八章共鳴歡愉之理

content:
要是永遠都數值碾壓過關 一開始會有數值之美 然後就感到毫無體驗 因為我只是在不斷重複一樣的操作 然後獲得同樣且必然的結果
但忘卻好玩就在於 遊玩過程不是必然的
如果必定會贏 那其實我什麼也沒有在玩
就像我把死抗堆到800 那其實已經不能算是死抗率了 因為我知道我死抗一定會成功 而且我會成功很多次
不死不休是我目前最喜歡的生涯成就沒有之一
要說有什麼小小的可惜的地方 就是我的不死不休是菲茵特疊出來的
那跟死抗只有2%的隊伍居然成功了一次 帶來的成就感是完全不一樣的
（好吧坦白說因為我是用菲茵特其實打到第七還六章左右我都沒有戰敗過，我根本不知道這個遊戲是會死的）那不死不休也只是不知不覺罷了⋯⋯
thread_title: 💭「处理中」忘卻篇第六章共鳴卡純白之帆 & 第八章共鳴歡愉之理
